_source: Acumen Fuse v8.11.0 export suite for Hard_File.mpp + Hard_File_updated.mpp, delivered by the operator 2026-07-08 in 00_REFERENCE_INTAKE/acumen_v8.11.0/ (repo-tracked, non-CUI build/reference input per CLAUDE.md). Values transcribed VERBATIM from 'Hard_File_Fuse - Metric History Report.xlsx' (workbook 'Hard_File_Fuse - 282 Activities', created 7/8/2026 by dpolitte), which carries both snapshots side by side: Hard_File (Time Now 7/7/2026) and Hard_File_updated (Time Now 8/11/2026). Corroborated by the Summary Metric and Quick Add Metrics reports in the same suite.
_note: Second independent ENGINE==FUSE oracle (the first is project2_5/fuse_exports_2026-06.json, ADR-0151). This one uniquely covers an ELAPSED IN-PROGRESS activity (needs-list D7): the updated snapshot carries exactly one normal in-progress to-go task (Normal Tasks (To-Go, In Progress) 0 -> 1), reproduced exactly. 15 metric values across the two snapshots reproduce Fuse UID-for-count exactly (test_fuse_hardfile_engine_equals_fuse); 3 divergences are recorded under _documented_divergences and asserted EXACTLY in the test rather than forced (Law 2).
_fixtures: Hard_File.mspdi.xml.gz / Hard_File_updated.mspdi.xml.gz — MPXJ-converted MSPDI of the intake .mpp files, gzipped (task names are the source file's own anonymized business-process strings; non-CUI).

negative_float.cause: Every engine offender carries the source tool's STORED Critical flag (stored_is_critical=True) but no stored TotalSlack: MPXJ's MSPDI conversion omits <TotalSlack> for these tasks (34/142 in Hard_File; UID 147 'Product Review COMPLETE' is a source-Critical milestone). The engine's effective_total_float (ADR-0010/0080) then falls back to the recomputed pure-logic CPM float, which reads negative on these constrained/leveled tasks where MS Project's stored slack (which Fuse reads) is 0. This is the documented stored-vs-recomputed CPM divergence, NOT a curve-fittable data typo: forcing a clamp to 0 would hide a genuine CPM-vs-MSProject difference. Recorded on the needs list for a deeper CPM-parity investigation (why the recompute diverges on these tasks — constraints/leveling/data-date).

missing_logic_updated.cause: On Hard_File the engine matches Fuse exactly (7=7); on the updated snapshot the engine finds 10 open-ended tasks vs Fuse's 8. Fuse's own components on the updated file (Missing Predecessors 0 + Missing Successors 7) do not themselves sum to its Missing Logic 8, so Fuse applies a metric-definition nuance (likely an LOE/start-milestone exclusion) not published in the export. Not forced pending Fuse's exact Missing Logic definition.

activities_with_duration_0.cause: The raw MSPDI carries ZERO non-milestone zero-duration non-summary tasks (all 25 zero-duration tasks are flagged Milestone=1), so the engine counts 0 by MS Project's own milestone flag. Fuse reports 1 under a distinct internal classification of 'Activities with Duration=0' not derivable from the exported flags. A 1-count divergence on a minor metric; documented, not forced.

missing_logic_updated23.cause: The engine's offender set is a strict SUPERSET of Fuse's: the extra UIDs (187, 400, 412) are COMPLETED/finished activities that are genuinely open-ended in the file. The 2026-07-09 Detailed Metric Reports exclude completed activities from Missing Logic, but Fuse's own earlier exports (Project2/Project5, ADR-0151, and Hard_File base 7=7) COUNT completed open ends — an incomplete-only engine variant would break those pinned oracles (4!=6, 5!=7). Operator direction 2026-07-09: keep the engine behavior (open-endedness is a structural property; completion does not repair logic), document the Fuse-side inconsistency.

invalid_forecast_dates_field_count.cause: Engine DCMA09 counts ACTIVITIES; Fuse's Metric History counts date FIELDS, so a task with both its start and finish in the past contributes 2 there (42 = these 21 activities x 2 on updated2). The Detailed Metric Report's per-activity X marks (what the engine is verified UID-exact against) confirm the activity set is identical.

_source2: Second delivery 2026-07-09: Hard_File_updated2.mpp / Hard_File_updated3.mpp + the Fuse v8.11.0 Detailed Metric Report workbooks in 00_REFERENCE_INTAKE/acumen_v8.11.0/ (repo-tracked, non-CUI). UID sets transcribed from the per-activity X marks of the Detailed Metric Reports; scalar values from the Metric History Report.

Hard_File_updated->Hard_File_updated2._source: Fuse Forensic Analysis Report change sheets (Normal rows; Fuse additionally lists summary rollup rows, which the engine's leaf-level diff correctly excludes — rollups are derivative of the leaf changes)

Hard_File_updated2->Hard_File_updated3._source: Fuse Forensic Analysis Report change sheets (Normal rows; Fuse additionally lists summary rollup rows, which the engine's leaf-level diff correctly excludes — rollups are derivative of the leaf changes)
